calendar.matters:
Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To Ccp § 583.250(A) For Failure To Serve Summons Within Three Years Of Commencement Of The Action; Motion To Quash Service And Vacate Default Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT F. COHEN, ESQ. of Law Officec of Robert F. Cohen, Tel No. 415-593-6672 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. AMY ANDERSON, ESQ. of Aroplex Law, Tel No. 415-866-4066, Tel No. 415-866-4066 appearing telephonically for Defendant Luke Duncan.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT LUKE DUNCAN'S Motion To Dismiss Pursuant To CCP Sec. 583.250(A) For Failure To Serve Summons Within Three Years Of Commencement Of The Action; Motion To Quash Service And Vacate Default Judgment, Defendant Luke Duncan's motion to dismiss, quash service and vacate the default judgment is denied. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is also denied. Since a default judgment was entered, a dismissal and quashing of service cannot occur unless and until the default judgment is set aside. Mr. Duncan has not provided a sufficient basis to set aside the default judgment. The motion is untimely per CCP 473 and there has been an insufficient showing of either extrinsic fraud or mistake and reasonable diligence. Moreover, even if Mr. Duncan had shown extrinsic fraud or mistake and had acted diligently, the filing of a satisfaction of judgment shows that setting aside the default judgment is a meaningless act since there is nothing left to litigate. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order for the Court's signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
